heading: Bureau of Land Management Foundation
label: 1748c Bureau of Land Management Foundation
notes: CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Department of the Interior, Environment, and Related Agencies Appropriations Act, 2017, and also as part of the Consolidated Appropriations Act, 2017, and not as part of the Federal Land Policy and Management Act of 1976 which comprises this chapter.

children.heading: Board
children.label: 1 Board
children.text: The term “Board” means the Board of Directors of the Foundation established under subsection (c).

children.heading: Foundation
children.label: 2 Foundation
children.text: The term “Foundation” means the Bureau of Land Management Foundation established by subsection (b)(1)(A).

children.heading: Public land
children.label: 3 Public land
children.text: The term “public land” has the meaning given the term “public lands” in .section 1702 of this title

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 4 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of the Interior.

children.heading: Wild free-roaming horses and burros
children.label: 5 Wild free-roaming horses and burros
children.text: The term “wild free-roaming horses and burros” has the meaning given the term in .section 1332 of title 16

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: There is established a foundation, to be known as the “Bureau of Land Management Foundation”.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: B Limitation
children.text: The Foundation shall not be considered to be an agency or establishment of the United States.

children.heading: Tax exemption
children.label: C Tax exemption
children.text: The Foundation shall be considered to be a charitable and nonprofit corporation under .section 501(c)(3) of title 26

children.label: A
children.text: to encourage, accept, and administer private gifts of money and real and personal property for the benefit of, or in connection with the activities and services of, the Bureau of Land Management;

children.label: B
children.text: to carry out activities that advance the purposes for which public land is administered;

children.label: C
children.text: to carry out and encourage educational, technical, scientific, and other assistance or activities that support the mission of the Bureau of Land Management; and

children.label: i
children.text: reclamation and conservation activities;

children.label: ii
children.text: activities relating to wild free-roaming horses and burros; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the stewardship of cultural and archeological treasures on public land.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Foundation shall be governed by a Board of Directors.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Board shall consist of not more than 9 members.

children.heading: Ex-officio member
children.label: ii Ex-officio member
children.text: The Director of the Bureau of Land Management shall be an ex-officio, nonvoting member of the Board.

children.heading: Citizenship
children.label: i Citizenship
children.text: A member appointed to the Board shall be a citizen of the United States.

children.heading: Expertise
children.label: ii Expertise
children.text: A majority of members appointed to the Board shall have education or experience relating to natural, cultural, conservation, or other resource management, law, or research.

children.heading: Diverse points of view
children.label: iii Diverse points of view
children.text: To the maximum extent practicable, the members of the Board shall represent diverse points of view.

children.heading: Date of initial appointment
children.label: 2 Date of initial appointment
children.text: Not later than 1 year after , the Secretary shall appoint the initial members of the Board.May 5, 2017

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), a member of the Board shall be appointed for a term of 6 years.

children.label: i
children.text: 1/3 of the members shall serve for a term of 2 years;

children.label: ii
children.text: 1/3 of the members shall serve for a term of 4 years; and

children.label: iii
children.text: 1/3 of the members shall serve for a term of 6 years.

children.label: i
children.text: not later than 60 days after the date of the vacancy;

children.label: ii
children.text: in the manner in which the original appointment was made; and

children.label: iii
children.text: for the remainder of the term of the member vacating the Board.

children.heading: Removal for failure to attend meetings
children.label: D Removal for failure to attend meetings

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: A member of the Board may be removed from the Board by a majority vote of the Board, if the individual fails to attend 3 consecutive regularly scheduled meetings of the Board.

children.heading: Requirements
children.label: ii Requirements
children.text: A vacancy as the result of a removal under clause (i) shall be filled in accordance with subparagraph (C).

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: E Limitation
children.text: A member of the Board shall not serve more than 12 consecutive years on the Board.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Board shall elect a Chairperson from among the members of the Board.

children.label: i
children.text: shall serve as Chairperson for a 2-year term; and

children.label: ii
children.text: may be reelected as Chairperson while serving as a member of the Board.

children.heading: Quorum
children.label: 5 Quorum
children.text: A majority of the voting members of the Board shall constitute a quorum for the transaction of business of the Board.

children.label: A
children.text: at the call of the Chairperson; but

children.label: B
children.text: not less than once each calendar year.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Serving as a member of the Board shall not constitute employment by the Federal Government for any purpose.

children.heading: Reimbursement
children.label: B Reimbursement
children.text: A member of the Board shall serve without pay, other than reimbursement for the actual and necessary traveling and subsistence expenses incurred in the performance of the duties of the member for the Foundation, in accordance with .section 5703 of title 5

children.label: A
children.text: appoint officers and employees in accordance with paragraph (9);

children.label: B
children.text: adopt a constitution and bylaws consistent with the purposes of the Foundation and this section; and

children.label: C
children.text: carry out any other activities that may be necessary to function and to carry out this section.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: No officer or employee may be appointed to the Foundation until the date on which the Board determines that the Foundation has sufficient funds to pay for the service of the officer or employee.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: B Limitation
children.text: Appointment as an officer or employee of the Foundation shall not constitute employment by the Federal Government.

children.heading: Prohibition on political activity
children.label: A Prohibition on political activity
children.text: The Foundation shall not participate or intervene in a political campaign on behalf of any candidate for public office.

children.label: i
children.text: the financial interests of the member of the Board, officer, or employee; or

children.label: I
children.text: is an officer, director, or trustee; or

children.label: II
children.text: has any direct or indirect financial interest.

children.label: A
children.text: shall have perpetual succession; and

children.label: B
children.text: may conduct business throughout the several States, territories, and possessions of the United States.

children.heading: Designated agent
children.label: A Designated agent
children.text: The Foundation shall at all times maintain a designated agent in the District of Columbia authorized to accept service of process for the Foundation.

children.heading: Service of process
children.label: B Service of process
children.text: The serving of notice to, or service of process on, the agent required under this paragraph, or mailed to the business address of the agent, shall be deemed to be notice to, or the service of process on, the Foundation.

children.heading: Seal
children.label: 3 Seal
children.text: The Foundation shall have an official seal, to be selected by the Board, which shall be judicially noticed.

children.label: A
children.text: to accept, receive, solicit, hold, administer, and use any gift, devise, or bequest, absolutely or in trust, of real or personal property, or any income from, or other interest in, the property;

children.label: B
children.text: to acquire by donation, gift, devise, purchase, or exchange, and to dispose of, any real or personal property or interest in the property;

children.label: C
children.text: to sell, donate, lease, invest, reinvest, retain, or otherwise dispose of any property or income from property, unless limited by the instrument of transfer;

children.label: D
children.text: to borrow money and issue bonds, debentures, or other debt instruments;

children.label: E
children.text: to sue and be sued, and complain and defend itself in any court of competent jurisdiction, except that the members of the Board shall not be held personally liable, except in a case of gross negligence;

children.label: i
children.text: to enter into contracts or other agreements with public agencies, private organizations, and persons; and

children.label: ii
children.text: to make such payments as may be necessary to carry out the purposes of the contracts or agreements; and

children.label: G
children.text: to carry out any activity necessary and proper to advance the purposes of the Foundation.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: For purposes of this section, an interest in real property shall include mineral and water rights, rights-of-way, and easements, appurtenant or in gross.

children.heading: Acceptance
children.label: B Acceptance
children.text: A gift, devise, or bequest of real property may be accepted by the Foundation, regardless of whether the property is encumbered, restricted, or subject to beneficial interests of a private person, if any current or future interest in the property is for the benefit of the Foundation.

children.heading: Declining gifts
children.label: C Declining gifts
children.text: The Foundation may, at the discretion of the Foundation, decline any gift, devise, or bequest of real property.

children.heading: Prohibition on condemnation
children.label: D Prohibition on condemnation
children.text: No land, water, or interest in land or water, that is owned by the Foundation shall be subject to condemnation by any State, political subdivision of a State, or agent or instrumentality of a State or political subdivision of a State.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: For the purposes of assisting the Foundation in establishing an office and meeting initial administrative, project, and other expenses, the Secretary may provide to the Foundation, from funds appropriated under subsection (j), such sums as are necessary for fiscal years 2017 and 2018.

children.heading: Availability of funds
children.label: B Availability of funds
children.text: Funds made available under subparagraph (A) shall remain available to the Foundation until expended for authorized purposes.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary may provide to the Foundation personnel, facilities, equipment, and other administrative services, subject to such limitations, terms, and conditions as the Secretary may establish.

children.heading: Reimbursement
children.label: B Reimbursement
children.text: The Foundation may reimburse the Secretary for any support provided under subparagraph (A), in whole or in part, and any reimbursement received by the Secretary under this subparagraph shall be deposited in the Treasury to the credit of the appropriations then current and chargeable for the cost of providing the services.

children.heading: Volunteers
children.label: f Volunteers
children.text: The Secretary may accept, without regard to the civil service classification laws (including regulations), the services of the Foundation, the Board, and the officers, employees, and agents of the Foundation, without compensation from the Department of the Interior, as volunteers for the performance of the functions under .section 1737(d) of this title

children.heading: Audits
children.label: 1 Audits
children.text: For purposes of , the Foundation shall be considered to be a private corporation established under Federal law.section 10101 of title 36

children.heading: Annual reports
children.label: 2 Annual reports
children.text: At the end of each fiscal year, the Board shall submit to Congress a report that describes the proceedings and activities of the Foundation during that fiscal year, including a full and complete statement of the receipts, expenditures, and investments.

children.heading: United States release from liability
children.label: h United States release from liability

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The United States shall not be liable for any debt, default, act, or omission of the Foundation.

children.heading: Full faith and credit
children.label: 2 Full faith and credit
children.text: The full faith and credit of the United States shall not extend to any obligation of the Foundation.

children.heading: Limitation on authority
children.label: i Limitation on authority
children.text: Nothing in this section authorizes the Foundation to perform any function the authority for which is provided to the Bureau of Land Management under any other provision of law.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: j Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated such sums as are necessary to carry out this section.
